yesterdayAnalysis: Solid execution of the rescheduled 10.2-mile long run at Carlsbad Run Club with HR 144 (well-controlled aerobic effort) earning 102 TSS — this was the first long run after a gap and sets proper endurance foundation. Combined with morning pool work (2.0mi total, 28 TSS), yesterday delivered 130 TSS. The run pacing appears conservative and appropriate given it's the first extended effort in this block.
todayRecommendation: Execute the planned SHORT easy brick run as scheduled — keep it truly easy (30-40min bike, 10-15min jog max) since yesterday's 10-miler was significant stress. This is a neuromuscular primer, not a load session, so respect the recovery need before Thursday's full rest day.
weekProgress: Week 8 of 10 shows 201 TSS through Tuesday (40% of 500 target with 4 days remaining including today). This is on-track given the back-loaded structure — Saturday's long ride and the completed brick Monday will carry the volume. Swim target (9000m) needs 5500m more across Friday/Sunday sessions.
keyInsight: Sleep quality remains inconsistent (68/100 avg, scores of 49 and 59 this week) during peak volume week — prioritize sleep hygiene tonight and tomorrow to maximize adaptation from the 10-mile run and prepare for Saturday's long ride.
